title: graph_nodes at capture and opt-in recall token budget
summary: Added the last two PRD aligned memory mechanisms within Kage's zero dependency design. CaptureInput.graphNodes lets the agent declare code graph node references at capture; capture stores them as deduped {relation:'refer
body:
Added the last two PRD-aligned memory mechanisms within Kage's zero-dependency design. CaptureInput.graphNodes lets the agent declare code-graph node references at capture; capture() stores them as deduped {relation:'references_code',to,evidence:'agent_capture'} edges (survives refresh, like supersede lineage). GraphInputs.maxContextTokens + boundContextBlock() give recall an opt-in hard token budget that drops trailing lower-priority sections (preamble/code-graph/memory kept first); default path unchanged. Wired into kage_capture/kage_learn (graph_nodes) and kage_recall (max_context_tokens) tools + CLI. Deliberately did NOT add SQLite graph store or ANN index — they break the no-API-key/zero-dep promise and need explicit owner sign-off.
Verified by: npm test (182 pass); tsc --noEmit clean
